_provenance.purpose: Contrast target for the discrimination control: problem_07 Q2 (M-estimator asymptotic normality) is a DIFFERENT theorem from Banach. Aligning it against the Banach base should yield ~no structural overlap => HIGH novelty, proving the detector discriminates (it does not always say 'instance'). Disjoint functor vocabulary from banach_case.json by construction.

target.name: problem_07-q2
target.text: Define the normalized error U_t = sqrt(n)(theta_hat_t - theta_t). Conditional on theta_hat_{t-1}, the samples at time t are i.i.d. from D(theta_hat_{t-1}), and by the classical M-estimator central limit theorem, sqrt(n)(theta_hat_t - T(theta_hat_{t-1})) converges in distribution to a Gaussian N(0, Sigma). Turning the conditional CLT into a marginal CLT by induction and the characteristic function method, using the tower property of expectation, U_t converges in distribution to N(0, V_t). The recursive variance satisfies V_t = grad_T(theta_{t-1}) V_{t-1} grad_T(theta_{t-1})^T + Sigma(theta_t), where Sigma is the sandwich covariance H^{-1} V H^{-1} with H the Hessian and V the gradient covariance.
